Pretty amazing, isn't it? So let me just quickly tell you my story about how I got started in the speaking business And I can tell you this, I really did not want to become a speaker when I was younger That wasn't my passion at that time, it is now I love the speaking business, I love teaching people just like you How to make more money, it's a lot of fun So let me quickly tell you my story then I'm going to bring up the blueprint here And be breaking down exactly what to do here So as you can see, this is a picture of me, I'm actually the middle child right there As a happy boy at a young age, I grew up in actually Ann Arbor, Michigan I had a great family and my parents actually sent me to a Christian school I had a great, great, great family I went to college at Eastern Michigan University and I actually got my degree in sports medicine I actually became a physical therapist, a sports trainer I had an amazing opportunity, I moved down to Tampa, Florida right here And I got my first big break, I actually started working in a hospital as a physical therapist Helping people learn how to walk again and move their extremities again after surgery It was an amazing, amazing job Then after one year of doing that, I got my first big break A professional football team called me up, I became a professional football trainer I got to travel the world, I was on ESPN TV, I was on NBC TV I had an amazing, amazing career and I won a championship in 2003 I followed my passions and my dreams But after one year winning the championship, when I was on top of the mountain When I was on top of the mountain, I had everything that I wanted Something extremely bad happened to me, something extremely so bad I started to get these back spasms in my back The pain was so severe, it ran all the way down my leg I couldn't stand or sit for more than ten minutes So picture this, imagine this, I went to see a team orthopedic surgeon And imagine this, I'm sitting in his office that day The doctor came strolling in, he put my MRI and my X-ray on the board And says, Dave, I see your promise right here I'm like, Doc, what's wrong with me? I'm in so much pain, I could barely get here I'm only 29 years old, what's going on? He says, Dave, I see your promise right here He says, Dave, you got to broke him back You got a paris fracture of L5S1 with a herniated disc I'm like, Doc, what does that mean? He looked at me that day and he says, Dave, there's nothing I can do for you I said, Doc, what do you mean? I'm only 29 years old, I'm physically fit What do you mean there's nothing you can do for me? He says, Dave, there's nothing I can do for you So at that present moment, I demanded a better life I told him I wanted to get surgery He explained to me, he says, Dave, I'm not sure if surgery will even work I'm not sure if you'll be able to walk again, function again I'm not sure if you'll be able to even work So I demanded surgery that day So picture this, five days later he did the surgery I actually took two surgeons to replace my back They actually cut me through the anterior part of my stomach They moved the organs out of the way and they screwed my back together They put a rod in the cage in my back at 29 years old Imagine this, I woke up in the recovery room, paralyzed In so much pain, I couldn't even feel my legs I was in so much pain I'm sitting there laying in my mirror thinking, what am I going to do now? What kind of career can I take? What kind of shift am I going to have to make in my business? Am I going to be able to walk again? And I remember leaving the hospital about a week later My parents picked me up from the hospital, left in a wheelchair And I went home to my parents' house And the next day I woke up in my parents' house They had a hospital bed there for me and I looked out their window Their screen didn't window, they had a pool out back A screen didn't pool out back And I said to myself, I said, Dave, if you can learn how to walk around that pool just one time You can learn how to walk again So I took my wheelchair out that day They gave me this full body cast that I put on, I took this cane And I started to walk around the pool just like this It took me about 20 minutes to get around that pool that first day The second day I walked around that pool twice The third day I walked around that pool three times By the following week I'm thinking to myself, what can I do now? What kind of career can I take? And that's when I decided that I wanted to get involved in real estate So with my full body cast on, long story short, bought my first house Flipped it and made $42,000 in real estate Just imagine and vision that Two months after surgery, no money, no credit, flipped the house, made $42,000 So then after doing that, I wanted to teach people what exactly how I did it And then that's when I created a company, an infomarketer company To teach people exactly how to do that So I created a company called foreclosuresdaily.com And what I created was a membership website That real estate investors could go to They'd pay $99 a month to get this information of foreclosures Well, I started developing this website And I got it running up where I was doing $100,000 a month residual Every single month I was making $100,000 residual off my membership website But I gotta tell you, that wasn't the best part of this whole thing And I'm gonna share with you in a moment a secret So all these people had this foreclosure list, but they didn't know what to do with it So I put my first foreclosure little workshop on, something like this And I did it at a Denny's restaurant Dustin was my marketer back then, made up some flyers We got about eight people to our first seminar at the Denny's restaurant And we did our first event at the restaurant I think we made $1,500 our first day And we were excited about that because back ten years ago that was a lot of money And then I figured out, man, this seminar business is a lucrative business You can make a lot of money in this business So fast forward we started doing more seminars And we started learning that it's better to do them not at restaurants But it's better to do them at hotels Because people's mindset when they come to buy is different at a hotel than it is at a restaurant So we started doing more events We got up to doing 50 seminars per month Building our team The following year after that, we got up to doing 100 seminars per month That year was 2007 where we made the Ink 500 magazine How many of you heard of the Ink 500 magazine? They want to be entertained You've got to get that in mind, because a lot of people want to teach, teach, teach, teach How many of you out there are teaching and teaching and teaching and not selling anything? How many of you have that maybe going on? That happens, and I'll share with you why that is And why that process doesn't work So we don't want to be trainers, we want to be inner-tainers And I'm going to share with you basically how to do that So we've got this whole process mapped out for you So I looked at Hollywood, and Hollywood does this They'll take an actor or speaker, he'll go to Hollywood A professional person writes their script and directs their movie Somebody who is an expert in script writing, expert in camera work, video work, screen work This actor goes to Hollywood and then plays the part, does that make sense? So you could take a below average actor, maybe Arnold Schwarzenegger Give him a great part and he'll do what? He'll win the Emmy, right? And some other great things too, right? So this is what we have to think about, when you see speakers, they're all scripted And this is what you have to understand, they'll memorize these scripts like an actor And when they're up on stage, they might seem like they're naturally discommunicating But it's all scripted word for word The reason that you want to be scripted in today's time Is because when somebody gets you on their stage or their webinar They're going to want what kind of results? Variable results or consistent results? What kind of results they want? And how do you get consistent results when you're scripted, right? Because if you're not scripted, you're going to be like this, okay? You can't do that And what will happen is after you get scripted, you're going to get that call that one time that says Hey, one of these speakers got sick, I got an opening for a webinar And you better be ready for it, correct? The definition of preparation meets opportunity So when you're prepared, right? Then you're going to get that opportunity I want to make sure you're prepared, does that make sense? So basically, that's what we do We take speakers and we direct their movie We help them figure out what they're going to sell, how they're going to sell it We basically produce their whole movie, okay? That's a subconscious. So we talked about the intro. The next thing that you want to do is strategy number two. You guys are going to really want to want this one. You've got to grab the attention of the audience. What most speakers do is the wrong strategy. They'll get up on their soapbox and they'll talk at people. Kind of like a football coach talking to kids. That's not the best strategy. You want to bring people into your conversation. What we're going to use is an NLP technique called Have You Ever Wondered? Write this down. Have you ever wondered? So you'll say something like this. Have you ever wondered what it would be like to be able to drive a massive amount of leads to your business? Have you ever wondered what it would feel like to be able to make money while you sleep? Have you ever wondered what it would feel like to get a thousand hours in one day? So what we did is we excited their imagination and then brought them into our conversation. So now they want to listen to us. Does that make sense? Who else has got a business or product sell-from-stage? Anybody else? What's your product? I would sell and rent a vacation home in the rental area. Sell and vacation and rent a home. So we'll start our conversation like this. Have you ever wondered what it would be like to take a dream vacation and spend more time with your family? Have you ever wondered what it would feel like to be able to go to Disney World, to see the world and be able to spend amazing time? Have you ever wondered what it would feel like to be able to have a beautiful place to stay on your vacation? So you excited your imagination and brought them into your conversation. Does that make sense? So now they want to listen to you. Another great thing that you want to understand, I talked about selling is in the subconscious. All selling is in the subconscious. So you want to get inside their minds, right? So I want to get inside your minds. I want you to understand this one thing. Once you control their subconscious, once you control them emotionally, then you're going to be able to do the sales process. The best way to control them emotionally is this. When you control their physiology, you control them emotionally. That means when they start listening to you and start taking and moving them around and controlling their body, you control them emotionally. Now, does anybody know what I did when I got all of you to stand up? Did you guys remember that? I got you all to stand up. Why did I do that? Okay, close. I control you, right? You did what? I got you to stand up. Your physiology got to stand up. You turned to here. You did all this. I was controlling your physiology, right? So when I control your physiology, I can do what? Control you? Emotionally, right? I'm in the motion. So that's what you want to do. So when you get up on stage, a great strategy I would do is I want to grab the attention of the audience, bring them into my conversation. I get them to stand up immediately, okay? Start. Pay attention to you taking orders from me. Write this down. Small commitments equal a big commitment. That's hypnosis. So I hit with this will do. So I got you to start taking small commitments. what do I do? Get rid of the resistance, right? Then I have you sit down. So it's the best way if you're speaking in front of anybody is get them to stand up, make a joke, control their physiology, and guess what? You got them, right? Now you've got them engaged, okay? So we talked about the introduction, grabbing their attention. You want to control the audience, excite their imagination, and you want to bring them into your conversation. The next thing you want to do is get them into what they call a yes state. All successful sales people know that seven yeses equals a sale, right? We want to get them into a yes state. Okay, let me ask this question. What is the number one reason why somebody comes to a seminar? What's the number one reason? Nope. What's the number one reason why you come to a seminar? To make more money. So what question can you ask? They're going to say yes to. Buy a show of hands. How many of you want to make more money? Put them up and say, oh, yeah. Right? Buy a show of hands. How many of you want me to show you how to create an extra 30,000 dollars in the next 30 days? Put them up and say, oh, yeah. Oh, yeah. Buy a show of hands. How many of you would like me to show you a proven step-by-step system that will virtually guarantee you more money? Put them up. Let me see them nice and high. Great. So we're going to get them what they call a yes state. Getting the audience to agree with you. This is very important. Your very first sale of the presentation is getting them to agree with you. The very first sale of your presentation is getting them to agree with you. Small agreements equals big agreements, right? If you can't get them to do this and agree with you, you're not going to be able to get them to do this, are you? So the very first thing is you've got to get them to agree with you. Now, when you're writing your presentations, you want to get three to four yes statements. Now, let's go ahead and I want to show you real quick. Let's just demonstrate. I'll do a timeout. You know, buy a show of hands. How many of you would like me to show you how to make more money? Put them up and say, oh, yeah. Oh, yeah. Okay, keep your hands up. How many people have their hands up? Not everybody, right? This will happen to you, right? Now, remember, we can't get them to do this. They're not going to do this. So we got to do it as a strategy to force the response, right? How many of you saw Dustin forcing the response yesterday? Did you guys see that? That's what he was doing. He had to get you small agreements, right? So what will happen is you say, how many of you want to make more money and half the room gets their hand up? We've got to use a strategy to get everybody to agree with us. That makes sense. So a guy by the name of Marshall Silver will do something like this. Buy a show of hands. How many of you want to make more money? Put them up and say, oh, yeah. Put them up. If your hand isn't up right now, go ahead and leave. Because what I'm about to share with you in a moment, now he smacks them that hard. Because he knows if he can't get them to do this, they're not going to run to the back of the room. Does that make sense? Now, that's not my personality. It might not be your personality. So my strategy is a little bit different. I'd say, you know, buy a show of hands. How many of you want to make more money? Put them up. Seriously. Friends, I flew all the way from Tampa, Florida to be here with you today. And Tony Robbins wanted me to share with you some amazing strategies. Seriously, how many of you want, like me, sure, I had to make more money? Look at my body language. Getting up on my tippy toes, causing me to pause. Because what you want to do is you want to give a command and then you want to get a response. That's how powerful it's got to be. It's got to be a command and a response. That's when you're taking control of the audience. Now, so we talked about getting them into a yes state. Small commitments equal a. Big commitment. Seven yeses equals a. When we have the force to response, if there's a resistance. And the very first sale of the presentation is... Getting them to agree with you, right? So, we talked about getting them into a yes statement. The next thing that we talked about here is this one. The next thing that we talked about here is this is one of the most important things is what they call framing. Framing is a very powerful, powerful persuasion technique. What has happened with human beings is human beings have somehow lost communication touch, connection touch. We want to beat around the bush. But the more direct you are, the more you're telling people what you're going to do to them, the more they're going to... listen. So, framing is a very, very powerful thing. So, framing is basically telling people what you're going to do to them. It's almost like the front page in the newspaper. The front page of the newspaper may say something like this. Brett Farr is going to retire. More on page 5C. What do we have to do? Go to 5C, right? Listen. They basically framed us or opened looped us. Oh, by the way, I forgot to tell you something. I talked to Robert Allen, and asked if I wanted to give you something very, very important. I wanted to tell you something super, super important. But let me go back to framing. Framing is basically very, very important. You can tell people what you're going to do to them. Do you want to know what Bob said to you guys? Now, he said, just have fun. But you can open loop people very, very powerfully and frame them. Now, as a hypnotist, what I'll do is I will frame people when I hypnotize them. So I'll do something like this. I'm going to tell them when I'm going to tell them. So I'll say something like this. In a moment, I'm going to begin counting backwards from 10 to 1. The moment I say the number 10, your body will begin to relax. So what am I doing? I'm doing what? I'm telling them what I'm going to do to them. So you want to create a slide in your webinar that you're going to say something like this. What you're about to discover today. In a moment, I'm going to share with you and teach you about PPC. Today, I'm going to talk to you about how you can drive massive amount of traffic. I'm also going to share with you here, it's a moment on this call today, is I'm going to show you how to get 100 leads per hour. In a moment, I'm also going to share with you today is how you can explode your business by driving traffic online. So what we're doing right there is we're doing what? We're hooking them, hooking them, hooking them and framing them for the conversation. Because one of the keys that you have to understand on your webinar is you've got to get them and hook them so they're going to listen to the webinar. So that is the four slide in your presentation is basically hooking them. So when you write your fourth slide, think about the front page of a newspaper. And you're going to have all your open loops and you're going to give them all these great promises of what they're going to get. Now the best way to hook somebody is make them a promise. So let me just do a demonstration. Mark's got a product that teaches people about how to get business credit. So if you're somebody who's looking to get money for your capital for your business, he's got a course business credit. So he'll say something like this on his webinar. So in a moment, what you're about to discover is how to get capital for your business. Today, I'm also going to talk to you about how that 90% of small businesses need capital for their business. I'm also going to share with you today of how you can get unlimited money for your business. I promise you two things on this call. And making a promise is like the biggest hook. I promise you this. Make a promise. I promise you this. And that is like, bam. Now you got them. Does that make sense? Are you getting this? Can you see yourself doing this? Would this help you? Imagine if you learned this five years ago where you'd be. Yeah, right? So, okay, so good. So we talked about framing. The next thing you're going to do to do in your presentation, you got to sell them on your topic. Why? Why real estate? Why day trading? Why PPC? Why be healthy? So you got to sell them on being, you got to sell them on your topic. Okay. Then the next thing you're going to sell them on is why now? You got to get in the stage to take action now, right? So why do they need to take action now? You got to sell them on that. Okay. Does that make sense? Because we got to get people into action, right? Okay. The next thing we're going to do is, is after that we're going to talk about credibility. Now let me ask you this question. If I was speaking on stage about real estate or let you say Donald Trump was speaking on stage about real estate, who would people listen to more? Donald Trump. Why? Why would we listen to Donald Trump more? Experience? What else? Successful. He's got great hair. Because he is the expert. And when the experts speak, people listen. Okay. So you want to be the what? Expert. So we're going to create a slide, your credibility slide. This is building non-braggingly building you up or your business up as the expert. That's the slide is here. Now how many, how many of you watch and saw what I just did over there? How many of you pick that up? One person, two people, three people. So you can use another great strategy. Some of you guys might be picking up some of these little things that I'm doing, but you can do what? You can talk about somebody, build them up in this space right here. I talked about who? And Donald Trump is a what? A very powerful expert, right? And you saw, you guys all saw them in that circle. What happens when I get into that space? Who do I become? So a little subconscious little selling you can do here. So you can talk about a person or an expert and you're going to get into their space. I don't know how many of you saw Dustin do it yesterday? Yeah. Pretty cool, wasn't it? I got to tell you when I first met Dustin, I'll tell you a story about how I met him. He was a 22 year old kid, 135 pounds that would talk like this. Real shy, couldn't talk and just do using this training and stuff. You can see how explosive and how amazing he is. And the same thing you guys can do too by using this. Okay.
